target.name: Dataminr
target.founder: Ted Bailey, Sam Hendel, Jeff Kinsey

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $4.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Applied Intuition
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $15B

data.narrative:
Dataminr, a Series F AI & Software company, is valued at $4.1B post-money and has raised $485M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, including 8 private companies (Applied Intuition at $15.0B, Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, Groq at $7.0B, SandboxAQ at $5.6B) and 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach applies a typical 1.3x multiplier to the last post-money valuation of $4.1B, implying $5.3B, reflecting expected value creation from Series F to the next institutional round. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company cohort yields $5.6B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $250.0B—the latter driven by outlier xAI at Series E. Both methods are applicable given the homogeneous sector focus and proximate stage positioning.

The triangulated valuation range is $4.5B (low), $5.5B (median), and $6.4B (high). This range reflects modest upside from current post-money but sits squarely within peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the dispersion within the peer set—particularly whether xAI and Applied Intuition represent achievable comparables for Dataminr's business model and TAM—and the absence of recent public-market pricing signals in the AI & Software sector.
